response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role on nearly every critical dimension. The role requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which the candidate does not possess at all. The required deep C expertise for embedded UNIX/Linux kernel development is largely absent — the candidate's primary languages are JavaScript, TypeScript, and D, with only incidental C/C++ usage. There is no evidence of experience in network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), L2/L3 networking, switch firmware, or Linux kernel development, all of which are central to this position.
strengths: Long career spanning 30+ years demonstrates sustained technical capability and adaptability across domains
Some embedded systems exposure (robotic arms at Altom, low-latency systems at Sociomantic) and Linux familiarity
English proficiency meets the documentation and email communication requirement
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is listed as a hard, non-negotiable requirement
Primary stack is JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js with minimal C programming; no demonstrated Linux kernel development or modification experience
Zero background in network security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, switch chips, NETCONF, SNMP) or embedded networking firmware

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
